differentials.name: Larynx location more anterosuperiorly at level of C3-C4
differentials.raw: Larynx location more anterosuperiorly at level of C3-C4

differentials.name: Conus elasticus (located 1 cm below glottis): very susceptible to edema
differentials.raw: Conus elasticus (located 1 cm below glottis): very susceptible to edema

differentials.name: Intercostal and diaphragmatic muscles weaker In acute upper airway obstruction obtain lateral and frontal soft-tissue radiographs of the neck in upright position. If unstable, a single upright soft-tissue lateral radiograph is usually sufficiently diagnostic.
differentials.raw: Intercostal and diaphragmatic muscles weaker In acute upper airway obstruction obtain lateral and frontal soft-tissue radiographs of the neck in upright position. If unstable, a single upright soft-tissue lateral radiograph is usually sufficiently diagnostic.

context: Airway Obstruction in Childhood Predisposing anatomy compared to adult: In acute lower airway obstruction → upright frontal CXR during inspiration with depiction of > 6 anterior ribs at mid-hemidiaphragmatic level + lateral neck radiograph and also expiratory CXR with possible foreign body aspiration. Nasopharyngeal Narrowing choanal atresia, choanal stenosis, encephalocele (a) Congenital: adenoidal enlargement (= pharyngeal tonsils narrow pharynx), polyps (b) Inflammatory: (c) Neoplastic: juvenile angiofibroma, rhabdomyosarcoma, teratoma, neuroblastoma, lymphoepithelioma (d) Traumatic: foreig
